title: ThermoPheno
text: Crop Phenology Simulation Model — Historical Analysis & Climate Change Impact Assessment ThermoPheno: thermal-time-based phenology model assessing crop growing season shifts climate change ThermoPheno models timing crop planting, maturity, harvest using daily temperature data growing degree day (GDD / thermal time) framework. runs self-contained R Shiny application supports historical climate analysis multi-scenario climate change projections.

title: ThermoPheno
text: Three thermal time modes — simple (linear), capped (plateau), triangular (heat-stress) Summer winter crop support — full vernalization winter dormancy logic cool-season cereals Automatic calibration — required thermal time estimated user-supplied historical baseline, lookup tables Dynamic planting date — planting triggers temperature conditions, fixed calendar dates Climate scenario comparison — upload multi-model / multi-scenario projections; app splits simulates group Rich visualisations — ridge/density plots, temperature ribbon charts, monthly cycle plots, annual boxplots, season-timing heatmaps

title: ThermoPheno
code: TT = 0                                              if T_mean ≤ T_base TT = T_mean - T_base                               if T_base < T_mean ≤ T_opt TT = (T_opt - T_base) × (T_max_cut - T_mean)      if T_opt < T_mean < T_max_cut          / (T_max_cut - T_opt) TT = 0                                              if T_mean ≥ T_max_cut

title: ThermoPheno
text: Source functions file call run_simulation() directly:
code: source("ThermoPheno_functions.R")  weather <- prepare_weather(read.csv("my_weather.csv"))  # Calibrate cal <- estimate_required_tt(   weather          = weather,   baseline_years   = 1990:2010,   planting_mmdd    = "04-15",   days_to_maturity = 140,   t_base           = 8,   t_opt            = 25,   t_max_cut        = 35,   tt_mode          = "triangular",   crop_type        = "summer" )  # Simulate all years results <- run_simulation(   weather              = weather,   crop_name            = "Maize",   required_tt          = cal$required_tt,   earliest_planting_mmdd = "03-15",   latest_planting_mmdd   = "05-31",   latest_harvest_mmdd    = "10-01",   t_base               = 8,   t_opt                = 25,   t_max_cut            = 35,   tt_mode              = "triangular",   crop_type            = "summer" )  print(results)

title: ThermoPheno
text: Development driven air temperature (photoperiod, radiation, water stress). Daily mean temperature arithmetic mean T_min T_max. Vernalization simple counter — de-vernalization warm spells modelled. Frost-kill logic included. growing season simulated independently (carry-years).

title: ThermoPheno
text: model grounded classical thermal time / growing degree day literature: McMaster & Wilhelm (1997). Growing degree-days: one equation, two interpretations. Agricultural Forest Meteorology, 87(4), 291–300. Wang & Engel (1998). Simulation phenological development wheat crops. Agricultural Systems, 58(1), 1–24. Bonhomme (2000). Bases limits using ‘degree.day’ units. European Journal Agronomy, 13(1), 1–10. full theoretical documentation including equations, crop parameter rationale, model assumptions, see ThermoPheno_Theory.docx.
